old_claim.note: Nanotechnology (Early Research) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because advanced ceramics is a broader materials lineage that does not require the later nanotechnology field label.

source_shape.source_locator: Nanotechnology history review locating the named nanotechnology field in the 1970s and later nanoscience development.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review places nanotechnology terminology after many postwar advanced-materials and ceramics developments.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Nanoscale ceramics exist, but the broad advanced-ceramics category can arise from chemistry, kiln/furnace control, and materials processing without depending on nanotechnology as a field.

ontology_before: Advanced ceramics depended on a later narrow subfield, which made the broad materials category chronologically fragile.
ontology_after: Advanced ceramics now keeps pottery and advanced chemistry as broader upstream prerequisites.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-07-semiconductor-energy-agriculture-chronology.json keeps this removed edge absent and documents the corrected dependency scope.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to mean nanostructured ceramics only.
A separate nanoceramics node were added and this dependency moved there.
A ceramics history source showed advanced ceramics as scoped here emerged only after nanotechnology.
short_reason: Advanced ceramics is broader than and not dependent on nanotechnology.
